description:
You are a mobile build engineer doing maintenance on an internationalisation (i18n) stage of a CI pipeline.  
Some legacy resource files were produced on Windows machines in ISO-8859-1, but the rest of the tool-chain (Gradle, Fastlane, etc.) now expects UTF-8.  
Your assignment is to convert those legacy files to UTF-8, place the converted copies in a dedicated directory, and record what you did in a log that the QA automation will later parse.

Initial layout (already present when your session starts):

/home/user/mobile_pipeline/  
├── i18n_source/  
│   ├── en_US.properties        (ISO-8859-1)  
│   ├── es_ES.properties        (ISO-8859-1)  
│   └── jp_JP.properties        (ISO-8859-1)  
└── README.txt                  (UTF-8, leave untouched)

Your tasks:

1. Create the directory /home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_utf8/ if it does not yet exist.  
2. Convert every “*.properties” file that lives **directly** inside /home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_source/ from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8.  
   • The converted file must keep the same base name and extension, and must be stored inside /home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_utf8/.  
   • The converted copy must use Unix line-endings (LF).  
   • Do NOT modify anything in the original i18n_source directory.  
3. Produce a conversion log file at /home/user/mobile_pipeline/encoding_conversion.log with one line per file converted.  
   • The log file must already exist (create it) and must be **appended** to if it exists.  
   • Format: CSV with *no* header row and five comma-separated fields in exactly this order:

     filename_relative_to_project_root,original_encoding,target_encoding,status,iso8601_timestamp

     Example row (for illustration only):  
     i18n_source/en_US.properties,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8,success,2024-03-12T15:04:05Z  

   • “status” must be “success” if and only if the conversion actually completed and the output file is present.  
   • Timestamp must be in UTC ISO-8601 format with a trailing “Z”.  
4. After conversion, verify that every file present in /home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_utf8/ is truly encoded in UTF-8 (automated tests will check this).  
5. Ensure the permissions on every new file you create are 0644 (-rw-r--r--) and that i18n_utf8/ has permission 0755 (drwxr-xr-x).  
6. Do NOT use sudo.

Hints (not commands):  
• iconv, file, recode, or Python can help with transcoding.  
• The `file` utility can be used to double-check encodings.  
• Remember to normalise line endings if the conversion tool does not automatically do so.

Outcome that the automated grader will test:

a. Directory /home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_utf8/ exists and contains exactly three files: en_US.properties, es_ES.properties, jp_JP.properties.  
b. All three new files are valid UTF-8 and have identical textual content (apart from line ending normalisation) to their ISO-8859-1 originals.  
c. /home/user/mobile_pipeline/encoding_conversion.log exists, is non-empty, and contains **exactly three** CSV lines that follow the specified format and end with “Z”.  
d. No files in /home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_source/ or README.txt are modified.  
e. File and directory permissions match the specification.

You should not need more than 5–10 terminal commands to finish everything.
truth:
Initial state before agent starts:

1. Three legacy ISO-8859-1 files:

/home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_source/en_US.properties
Content (ISO-8859-1, CRLF line endings):
greeting=Hello\r\nfarewell=Goodbye\r\ncurrency=Dollar\r\n

/home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_source/es_ES.properties
Content (ISO-8859-1, CRLF line endings):
greeting=Hola\r\nfarewell=Adiós\r\ncurrency=Euro\r\n

/home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_source/jp_JP.properties
Content (ISO-8859-1, CRLF line endings):
greeting=こんにちは\r\nfarewell=さようなら\r\ncurrency=円\r\n
(These Japanese characters are represented using the byte sequence for ISO-8859-1 that will appear as “??????” when viewed raw; file(1) will nevertheless report ISO-8859-1 text.)

2. Permissions:
   i18n_source/       : 0755
   each *.properties  : 0644
   mobile_pipeline/   : 0755
   README.txt         : 0644

3. README.txt (UTF-8):
This pipeline contains localisation resources.
Do not edit files in i18n_source/ manually.

The grader will perform these checks after agent completion:

A. Directory /home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_utf8/ exists and has permission 0755.
B. Files in /home/user/mobile_pipeline/i18n_utf8/:
   - Same filenames as originals.
   - Permission 0644.
   - `file --brief --mime-encoding` reports "utf-8".
   - Content identical to originals once originals are converted to LF endings and interpreted as ISO-8859-1 then re-encoded to UTF-8.
C. /home/user/mobile_pipeline/encoding_conversion.log:
   - Exists, permission 0644.
   - Exactly three non-blank lines ending with newline characters.
   - Each line matches regex:
     ^i18n_source\/(en_US|es_ES|jp_JP)\.properties,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8,success,[0-9T:\-]{19}Z$
   - Order of lines is not important.
D. None of the three source *.properties files and README.txt have changed (mtime and checksum).
name: task_000000_32af4c91
